Package com.dtwthalion.ttio.protection

Class CipherSuite

java.lang.Object
com.dtwthalion.ttio.protection.CipherSuite
public final class CipherSuite extends Object
Cipher-suite catalog and algorithm-dispatched parameter helpers (v0.7 M48).

Pre-v0.7, encryption / signing / key-wrap APIs accepted an implicit fixed algorithm (AES-256-GCM for bulk, HMAC-SHA256 for signatures, AES-KW-style wrap for KEK). Key sizes and nonce lengths were hardcoded module-level constants.

v0.7 M48 generalises the public API with an algorithm parameter backed by this catalog. The intent was to shape the parameter hole so M49's post-quantum binding is a pure plug-in — no API change — once ML-KEM-1024 / ML-DSA-87 are ready.

v0.8 M49 activates the PQC entries. "ml-kem-1024" (FIPS 203) and "ml-dsa-87" (FIPS 204) transition from RESERVED to ACTIVE. The Java implementation uses Bouncy Castle 1.79+ as the PQC provider (see PostQuantumCrypto). Python and Objective-C use liboqs instead — the Java path is different because liboqs's Java bindings are immature and BC 1.79+ ships FIPS-compliant PQC natively. See docs/pqc.md for the discrepancy rationale.

"shake256" remains reserved in v0.8 (no consumer yet in the protection APIs).

Design note (binding decision 39): CipherSuite is a static allow-list, not a plugin registry. Adding a new algorithm is a source-code change. Runtime registration would let callers push FIPS-unapproved algorithms through production code; that complexity is deferred to v0.8+.

Cross-language equivalents: ObjC TTIOCipherSuite, Python ttio.cipher_suite.

Since:
0.7

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    CipherSuite.Category
    Category tag for catalog entries.
    static final record 
    CipherSuite.Entry
    Catalog entry.
    static final class 
    CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException
    Thrown when a key's length does not match the selected algorithm.
    static enum 
    CipherSuite.Status
    Status tag; only CipherSuite.Status.ACTIVE algorithms may be used for encrypt / sign / wrap operations.
    static final class 
    CipherSuite.UnsupportedAlgorithmException
    Thrown when a caller specifies an algorithm not in the catalog, or one with status RESERVED (activates in a later milestone).

    Description

    allEntries()

    True iff algorithm is listed in the catalog, including reserved entries.

    True iff algorithm is a known catalog entry with status ACTIVE.

    keyLength(String algorithm)

    publicKeySize(String algorithm)
     
    static int
    tagLength(String algorithm)
     
    static void
    validateKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
    Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key does not match the algorithm's required length.
    static void
    validatePrivateKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
    Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key is not the right length for algorithm's private key (KEM decapsulation / signing).
    static void
    validatePublicKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
    Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key is not the right length for algorithm's public key (KEM encapsulation / signature verification).

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • isSupported

      public static boolean isSupported(String algorithm)
      True iff algorithm is a known catalog entry with status ACTIVE. Reserved entries return false.

    • isRegistered

      public static boolean isRegistered(String algorithm)
      True iff algorithm is listed in the catalog, including reserved entries. Useful for error messages that distinguish 'unknown' from 'not yet implemented'.

    • category

      public static CipherSuite.Category category(String algorithm)

    • keyLength

      public static int keyLength(String algorithm)
      Returns:
      the fixed key length in bytes, or -1 for variable-length keys (HMAC).

    • nonceLength

      public static int nonceLength(String algorithm)
      Returns:
      the nonce / IV length in bytes. Zero for non-AEAD primitives. Replaces hardcoded IV_BYTES = 12 constants.

    • tagLength

      public static int tagLength(String algorithm)
      Returns:
      the tag / signature length in bytes.

    • validateKey

      public static void validateKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
      Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key does not match the algorithm's required length. Raise CipherSuite.UnsupportedAlgorithmException for reserved or unknown algorithms.

      Asymmetric algorithms (KEM / Signature) raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException directing the caller to validatePublicKey(java.lang.String, byte[]) / validatePrivateKey(java.lang.String, byte[]); this keeps role confusion out of the symmetric-focused call sites (pre-M49 callers pass only symmetric keys here).

    • validatePublicKey

      public static void validatePublicKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
      Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key is not the right length for algorithm's public key (KEM encapsulation / signature verification). Symmetric algorithms raise. @since 0.8

    • validatePrivateKey

      public static void validatePrivateKey(String algorithm, byte[] key)
      Raise CipherSuite.InvalidKeyException if key is not the right length for algorithm's private key (KEM decapsulation / signing). Symmetric algorithms raise. @since 0.8

    • publicKeySize

      public static int publicKeySize(String algorithm)
      Returns:
      asymmetric public-key length in bytes. Raises for symmetric algorithms. @since 0.8

    • privateKeySize

      public static int privateKeySize(String algorithm)
      Returns:
      asymmetric private-key length in bytes. Raises for symmetric algorithms. @since 0.8

    • allEntries

      public static List<CipherSuite.Entry> allEntries()
      Returns:
      all catalog entries (active + reserved).
